title: arenaria tetraquetra var. brevifolia
intro: Arenaria tetraquetra var. brevifolia, commonly known as Sea Campion, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) and is native to coastal areas in Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Sea Campion include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to aid in the healing of wounds and reduce inflammation. Sea Campion can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, which can be used topically or ingested to reap its medicinal benefits.
